message:
fix(ft-l9mxa): race HTTP client inner call with cx-cancel watcher (tick 387)

ft-l9mxa was filed tick 381 to track the finding from tick 380's
snapshot: DistributedHttpClient's response-read path didn't observe
mid-flight cx-cancel. This tick implements fix option (1) from the
bead description: race the inner HTTP call against a cx-cancel
watcher using crate::runtime_compat::select!.

Added:
1. `race_with_cx_cancel<F>` helper in distributed.rs — takes the
   inner HTTP future and runs it in a select! against a cancel
   watcher that polls cx.is_cancel_requested() every 50ms.
2. Both `DistributedHttpClient::get` and `::post` now:
   a. Pre-flight check: return Err(Cancelled) immediately if cx
      is already cancelled at entry.
   b. Wrap inner call in race_with_cx_cancel.

Updated:
3. Tick 380 snapshot test (`distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`):
   - Renamed the doc-comment history section to reflect the fix.
   - Tightened assertions:
     * elapsed < 1s (was < 15s before fix, the safety ceiling)
     * result = Ok(Err(ClientError::Cancelled)) exactly
       (was "any error variant, just don't hang")
   - Removed the `outer_timeout_result=` print since the outer
     timeout should no longer be observed firing.

Observed behavior (before vs after):

  Before ft-l9mxa fix:
    elapsed = 10.02s (the test's defensive outer timeout fired)
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

  After ft-l9mxa fix:
    elapsed = ~70-75ms (one 50ms poll cycle + task scheduling)
    result = Ok(Err(ClientError::Cancelled))

Full check script re-run: all 6 runs PASS, 82/82 tests total. No
other tests broke from the policy change — cancel-watcher race is
invisible to existing tests that don't fire mid-flight cancel.

Closes ft-l9mxa.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
